topics.title: Emerging AI Startups
topics.summary:
Based on my search results, here are the most important news items about emerging AI startups from the last 24 hours:

## Key News About Emerging AI Startups (Last 24 Hours)

### 1. **CB Insights AI 100 List Released**


CB Insights named its tenth annual AI 100 on May 5, 2026, showcasing the 100 most promising private artificial intelligence companies in the world.

 

This year's cohort spans autonomous security operations, humanoid robots, and domain-specific AI for healthcare and financial services, with a focus on startups showing "proof of real traction outside a demo environment."



### 2. **Rhino Federated Computing Recognition**


Rhino Federated Computing was named to CB Insights' AI 100 list

, gaining recognition as a leader in privacy-native, federated AI. 

The company enables organizations in highly-regulated industries to unlock the full value of their data without moving it, while also protecting proprietary models and intellectual property.



### 3. **Alex AI Makes Industry First**


Alex AI was named to CB Insights' AI 100 list on May 5, 2026, becoming the first recruiting software company to be featured on the AI 100.

 

The company builds AI recruiting agents for enterprise talent teams and is trusted by Fortune 100s, Big Four accounting firms, and the largest recruiting agencies.



### 4. **Sierra Raises Nearly $1 Billion**


Artificial intelligence startup Sierra is raising nearly a billion dollars in a new funding round

, founded by OpenAI chairman and former Salesforce co-CEO Bret Taylor. 

Sierra topped $150 million in annual recurring revenue in eight quarters

, with the company selling AI customer service agents.

### 5. **AI Startup Funding Momentum Continues**


The AI 100 companies have raised $10.9B in equity funding over time, including more than $2B in 2026 so far (as of 4/27/2026).

 This reflects continued strong investor appetite for emerging AI companies despite the market already being extremely active in Q1 2026.

**Sources:** CB Insights (GlobeNewswire, PRNewswire), CNBC

topics.title: Latest AI news
topics.summary:
I need to search for more specific recent news from the past 24 hours, as the results show mostly general AI news pages rather than specific recent stories.

Based on the search results, I now have enough information about recent AI news from the last 24 hours. Let me compile the key points with proper citations.

## Latest AI News - Key Developments from the Last 24 Hours

Here are 4-5 important AI news stories from May 6, 2026:

### 1. **OpenAI Releases GPT-5.5 Instant as Default ChatGPT Model**



OpenAI released GPT-5.5 Instant on May 5 as the new default model for ChatGPT, replacing GPT-5.3 Instant. The update focuses on reducing hallucinations in high-stakes domains such as law, medicine, and finance while maintaining low latency.

 

It builds on the broader GPT-5.5 release from last month, which boosted coding and knowledge work, scoring 81.2 on the AIME 2025 math test (up from 65.4) and 76 on the MMMU-Pro multimodal benchmark (up from 69.2).



**Source:** Tech Startups

### 2. **AMD Surges on Strong AI Chip Demand**



Advanced Micro Devices CEO Lisa Su told CNBC on Wednesday that her massive forecast revision was due to the demand surge for central processing units led by the growth of agentic artificial intelligence.

 

AMD shares popped 13% after the chipmaker issued strong guidance.



**Source:** CNBC

### 3. **AWS Introduces AI Agents for Virtual Desktops**



Amazon Web Services introduced a preview feature that allows AI agents to access and operate WorkSpaces virtual desktops using assigned identities. AWS said unique identities make it easier to track agent behavior.

 

Enterprise AI agents are starting to enter real work environments, raising new questions about identity, permissions, and audit trails.



**Source:** Tech Startups / Wall Street Journal

### 4. **ReFiBuy Raises $13.6M for AI-Optimized E-Commerce**



Raleigh-based ReFiBuy raised $13.6 million in seed funding to help e-commerce companies make product data more visible to AI tools such as ChatGPT and Claude. The company wants brands to prepare for a shopping world where AI agents guide product discovery.



**Source:** Tech Startups / Axios

### 5. **Anthropic Releases Financial Sector AI Agents**



Anthropic has released ten preconfigured AI agents for the financial sector that are designed to automate typical tasks performed by investment banks, asset managers and insurers.



**Source:** LLM Stats

topics.title: New AI models
topics.summary:
Based on the search results, I found limited specific announcements from the last 24 hours. Let me search for more recent updates.

Based on the search results, I can see news from the past few days but limited breaking news specifically from the last 24 hours. Let me provide you with the most important recent developments:

## Most Important AI Model News from the Last 24 Hours (May 5-6, 2026)

Based on available sources, here are the key developments:

### 1. **IBM's Major Enterprise AI Expansion (May 5, 2026)**


IBM announced its most comprehensive expansion of enterprise AI and hybrid cloud management capabilities at its annual Think conference, unveiling the next generation of IBM watsonx Orchestrate for multi-agent orchestration, IBM Confluent for real-time data to AI, IBM Concert platform for intelligent operations, and IBM Sovereign Core for operational independence.



### 2. **Apple Intelligence Opening to Third-Party AI Models (May 6, 2026)**


Apple is bringing AI model choice to Apple Intelligence in iOS 27, letting users swap in Claude, Gemini, or other third-party models across Siri, Writing Tools, and Image Playground via a new "Extensions" system.

 

This will let iPhone users choose from multiple third-party models from companies like Google and Anthropic, including the ability to set custom voices in Siri depending on which external model is responding.



### 3. **Anthropic's Financial Sector AI Agents**


Anthropic released ten preconfigured AI agents for the financial sector that are designed to automate typical tasks performed by investment banks, asset managers and insurers.



### 4. **Chinese Open-Weight Coding Models Surge**


Four Chinese labs released open-weights coding models inside a 12-day window: Z.ai's GLM-5.1, MiniMax M2.7, Moonshot's Kimi K2.6, and DeepSeek V4 all landed at roughly the same capability ceiling on agentic engineering at meaningfully lower inference cost than the Western frontier.

 

None costs more than a third of Claude Opus 4.7.



### 5. **Meta's New Proprietary Model - Muse Spark**


Meta unveiled Muse Spark, its first flagship large language model built under Chief AI Officer Alexandr Wang's newly formed Superintelligence Labs, delivering competitive performance on multimodal perception, reasoning, health, and agentic tasks at a fraction of the compute cost of its older Llama 4 mid-size variant.



**Note:** The AI industry is moving rapidly with most major model releases (GPT-5.5, Claude Opus 4.6-4.7, Gemini 3.1, Grok 4.20) occurring in recent weeks rather than in the specific last 24 hours.

topics.title: New announcements from leading tech companies
topics.summary:
Based on the latest tech news from the past 24 hours, here are the key announcements from leading tech companies:

## Key Tech Announcements (Last 24 Hours)

### 1. **Samsung Hits $1 Trillion Valuation**


Samsung achieved a significant milestone with strong investor confidence in its semiconductor and AI-related businesses, joining the exclusive club of trillion-dollar companies alongside Apple, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla, and TSMC.



### 2. **OpenAI Launches GPT-5.5 Instant**


On May 5, OpenAI released GPT-5.5 Instant as the new default model for ChatGPT, replacing GPT-5.3 Instant, with a focus on reducing hallucinations in high-stakes domains such as law, medicine, and finance while maintaining low latency.



### 3. **Anthropic Deploys AI Agents for Financial Services**


Anthropic rolled out 10 AI agents built for banks, insurers, asset managers, and fintech companies, with tools capable of handling tasks such as pitchbook drafting, financial statement review, credit memo preparation, and compliance escalation.



### 4. **Major AI Deployment Ventures from OpenAI and Anthropic**


OpenAI announced Monday that it raised $4 billion for a venture known as The Deployment Company, designed to get businesses to adopt its artificial intelligence tools, with the deal valuing the company at $10 billion and partners getting access to more than 2,000 portfolio companies and clients.

 

OpenAI rival Anthropic also announced Monday the launch of its own new venture focused on selling AI tools to enterprise companies, in partnership with Goldman Sachs, investment firm Blackstone and private equity group Hellman & Friedman, helping companies embed Anthropic's Claude AI model into their businesses.



### 5. **Pentagon AI Contracts with 8 Tech Giants**


The Department of Defense announced Friday an agreement with eight major technology companies to use their artificial intelligence tools in its classified networks, with companies involved including Elon Musk's SpaceX, ChatGPT-maker OpenAI, Google, Microsoft, Nvidia, Amazon Web Services, Oracle and Reflection.
